atom.desc: Able to define atom and a molecule

lewis_dot.desc: Able to create of the Lewis Dot Structure models.

bonds.desc: Define polar, non-polar, and covalent bonds.

molecules.desc: be able to define a molecule

systems.desc: Able to define a system, open/closed systems, and isolated systems

mass.desc: Understand the concept of mass versus weight.

lawOfUnivGrav.desc: Be able to use newton's law of gravitation (F = G\frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2})

COM.desc: Understand how to find the center of mass of an object

gravitational-field.desc: Be able to understand how larger masses form gravitational fields.

pva.desc: Understand the basics of position, velocity, and acceleration.

motion_diagrams.desc: Understand how to read and create 1D motion diagrams

fma.desc: The relationship between force, mass, and acceleration

acc.desc: Able to calculate and define acceleration

force.desc: Able to calculate and define force

newton_meters.desc: Relationship between Newtons, newton meters, and joules

equal_but_opposite.desc: Understand and apply newtons third law

si_units.desc: Understand SI units and their importance

greek-alpha.desc: Understand and notetate basic greek letters in capital and lowercase

atomic-weight.desc: Define atomic weight (average mass of all atoms of that element)

read-periodic.desc: Be able to read the basic facts from a periodic table

atomic-number.desc: Define atomic number, importance in isotopes, and locate it on an element on the periodic table

chemical-symbols.desc: Be able to write simple chemical symbols.

energy_heat_trans.desc: Understand how much energy is used in a heat transfer, and what factors it depends on

energy-forms.desc: An overview of different forms of energy

work.desc: Define work as the process of a change in energy and calculate its units relative to energy

chem_energy.desc: Intro to chemical energy and calories. Relates to chem_reations

electric_energy.desc: Intro to Electricity and electric energy.

kinetic_energy.desc: Able to define and use the formula to calculate kinetic energy of some object

grav_pot_energy.desc: Able to calculate and define gravitation potential energy, specifically on earth

conservation_energy.desc: Understand that energy is conserved in all forms; the total energy of an isolated system remains constant.

work_energy_theorem.desc: Able to relate work and energy

stats_sample.desc: Identify what a sample is in statistics

stats_mean.desc: Define what a mean is in statistics

stats-histogram.desc: Construct a histogram based on a set of date

csv.desc: Understand how to use and interperet a CSV file, and how it is structured.

python_basics.desc: A chapter introducing basic syntax of Python in preparation for the rest of the sequence.

sets_logic.desc: Understand the chapter on logic. This includes logical symbols, booleans, intersection, truth tables, venn diagrams, complements, subsets, cardinality, and contrapositives.

proofs.desc: Understand mathematical proofs, specifically direct proofs, contraposition, contradiction, and proof vocabulary.

conductor.desc: Understand what a conductor is, and what a super conductor is.

coulomb_amps.desc: Understand the connection between coulumbs and amps

dc.desc: Define the basics of a direct current

watts.desc: Define watts, note other equivalent units

capacitor.desc: Understand what a capacitor is and how it works

resistors-series.desc: Define what it means for components to be in series

laws-kirchhoff.desc: Understand Kirchhoff's Voltage and Current Laws

resistors-parallel.desc: Define what it means for components to be in parallel

fertilizer.desc: Understand how fertilizer works and grows plants

concrete.desc: Understand the process of concrete development

metals.desc: Define and understand a metal in depth

python_advanced.desc: An advanced chapter introducing functions, classes, error handling, and basic graphing with Matplotlib.
python_advanced.chap_title: A deeper look at Python syntax

recursion.desc: Be able to define recursion, write recursive algorithms, prevent infinite loops, and write inductive proofs for divide and conquer algorithms
recursion.chap_title: A deeper look at Python syntax

angle.desc: Define and understand what angles are

rad.desc: Understand the unit radians and convert to degrees

types-angle.desc: Define obtuse, acute, supplementary, and complementary angles

triangle.desc: Understand the properties of a triangle

types-triangle.desc: Understand the types of a triangle: equilateral, isosceles, scalene

pythagorean_theorem.desc: Understand and use the pythagorean theorem

hypotenuse.desc: Identify the hypotenuse of a triangle

distance_formula.desc: Understand the basics of the distance formula using the hypotenuse

congruence.desc: Understand the types of triangle congruence

circles.desc: Understand circles, their area, radius vs diameter, circumferences + area

sector.desc: Define a sector in context of a circle

tangent.desc: Define tangent in terms of a circle. Define secant in terms of a circle.

math-function.desc: Understand and define a function, and its domains/ranges. Differentiate from relations. Be able to use functions to find their input/outputs.

math-variable.desc: Understand and define a variable (sometimes referred to as a parameter) in terms of graphing and functions

math-graph.desc: Understand and define a graph. Be able to use desmos as a graphing simulator.

math-invf.desc: Understand the conversion between functions and their inverses.

math-eo.desc: Understand and differentiate even versus odd functions

math-piecewise.desc: Understand how to define and restrict piecewise functions.

sine.desc: Introduction to the sine function and its graph

cosine.desc: Introduction to the cosine function and its graph

trig_derivatives.desc: Understand how to derive and use trig derivative functions, specifically with sin and cosine

trig_functions.desc: Understand the relationships with all of the trig functions

trig_integrals.desc: Understand the area under the curve as a trig integral, and integrate the trig functions

sum_diff_formulas.desc: Understand use of sum and difference formulas

double_half_angle_ids.desc: Understand use of double angle and half angle identities

unit circle.desc: Understand the unit circle, how to locate different coordinates on it, and how to find side length values on it

volume-solids.desc: Calculate the volume of different solids, including pyramids, cylinders, spheres, and unique shapes

conic-sections.desc: Understand and calculate, and graph conic sections

vectors.desc: Understand a vector and its properties in depth

vector_add_sub.desc: Able to do vector addition and subtraction

charge.desc: Be able to define and understand the concept of charge. Use point charges to simulate different charges and charge fields within an area.

electric-field.desc: Understand how an electric field generates, where it points, and how it relates to Coulomb's law.

dipole.desc: Be able to understand and define dipoles and dipole moments

law-gauss.desc: Be able to understand Gauss's law. Find it simply without using the integral form

cont_charge_distribution.desc: Define continuous charge distributions and use them to find charge and electric fields.

electric-potential.desc: Understand electric potential and electric potential energy, and how calculus could be used to alter it.

intro_integration.desc: Basics of integration using physics concepts

calc_velocity.desc: Calculating velocity given acceleration and initial velocity

calc_accel.desc: Calculating position given given a graph

air_resistance.desc: Define air resistance (the video is a very good explanation)

free_fall.desc: Understand free fall as a force concept

distance_displacement.desc: Understand the distance between distance vs displacement

projectile_motion.desc: Understand projectile motion and independence of motion. Topics include topics thrown horizontally, basic escape velocity and newton's cannon, and objects launched at an angle.

fbd.desc: Be able to understand, interpret, and draw free body diagrams. Review all digital resources videos!

inclines.desc: Understand how to calculate force, acceleration, and kinematic variables for objects going up or down inclines. Understand how friction effects the net Force in both x and y.

consv_momentum.desc: Understand collisions and conservation of momentum

com_explosions.desc: Understand how momentum relates to the path of COM and how explosions effect that motion.

boat_terminology.desc: Define a boat's hull, port, deck, starboard,bow, stern, and methods of motion

rudder.desc: Understand and define the use of a rudder

sails.desc: Understand how sails work, attributes of the wind force and how wind pressure works

pitch-yaw-roll.desc: Define pitch yaw and roll as the 3 axes of rotation

siphon.desc: Understand how a siphon works, and how it works in a toilet

biases.desc: Understand types and influences of biases

solving_quadratics.desc: Understanding how to solve quadratic equations

quadratic_formula.desc: Apply the quadratic formula to find the roots of the equation

sequences1.desc: Understand sequences. Define their properties, arithmetic vs geometric sequences, and how to find the common factor or difference.

exponential_growth.desc: Calculate exponential growth and understand exponents

log_properties.desc: Understand how to use logarithm properties

half-life.desc: Understand the concept of a half life

inverse_trig.desc: Understand and calculate all inverse trig functions

bar_chart.desc: Be able to graph and interpret a bar chart

line_graph.desc: Be able to graph and interpret a line graph

pie_chart.desc: Be able to graph and interpret a pie chart

scatterplot.desc: Be able to graph and interpret a scatterplot

dataviz.desc: Be able to understand graphs and data distributions

dot.desc: Understand and multiply vectors using the dot product

manufacturing_methods.desc: Understand various manufacturing methods and their benefits/drawbacks

sound.desc: Understand how sound and sound waves are made

pitch.desc: Define pitch of a sound wave. Videos above talk about pitch.

frequency.desc: Define frequency of a sound wave. Videos above talk about frequency.

ac_transformer.desc: Use of Transformer to Change Voltage of AC

ac_three_phase_system.desc: Understand the purpose of the 3 phase system in AC

electromagnetic_induction.desc: Electromagnetic induction is the process by which a changing magnetic field induces an electromotive force (emf) in a conductor.

param_eq: overview.desc: Understand how parametric equations describe curves using a parameter.

param_eq: interpret.desc: Interpret x = f(t) and y = g(t) as a single curve in the plane.

param_eq: elim.desc: Eliminate the parameter to convert parametric equations to rectangular form.

param_eq: sketch.desc: Sketch parametric curves and determine direction of motion.

param_eq: restrict.desc: Understand how restrictions on t affect the portion of the curve that is traced.

vector_function.desc: Understand how to calculate vector valued functions

circular_motion.desc: Understand the concepts of circular motion and their equations. Identify uniform circular motion and non-uniform circular motion.

ghg.desc: Understand the greenhouse gas effect caused by radio waves

photon.desc: Understand the purpose of photons and how they form. Understand their frequency, the photoelectric effect, and photon momentum.

ellipses.desc: Use the reflective property of ellipses and elliptical orbits

refraction.desc: Understand refraction and use Snell's Law.

convex_concave.desc: Be able to define mirrors and lenses as convex or convex

lens.desc: Understand the purpose of a lens

refractive_index.desc: Understand the purpose of a refractive index and calculate it

oscillations-springs.desc: Understand the fundamentals of oscillations or repeated motion, SHM, springs fundamentals.

orbit.desc: Understand what causes something to orbit, differences in weight, and geosyncronous orbits.

escape_velocity.desc: Understand escape velocity and how to calculate it

polar_coordinate.desc: Understand how to plot points, change coordinate systems, and create basic shapes in polar

torque.desc: Understand the concept of torque of a rigid body around a pivot

moment_of_inertia.desc: Understand the moment of inertia, sometimes referred to as the moment

rotNewt2ndLaw.desc: Understand the rotational equivalent of newton's second law, relating torque, angular acceleration and moments

rockets.desc: Understand how rockets fly, how their engines work, their propulsion, and staging

long lat.desc: Define longitude and latitude as measures of geographic coordinate system

haversine.desc: Understand and use the haversine formula to find the distance between two points on a sphere

tides.desc: Understand how the moon effects tide

orbital_calendars.desc: Understand how often different orbits occur, and the earth sun and moon triangle

eclipses_and_phases.desc: Understand the moon's phases and eclipses

phases.desc: Understand the phases of the moon

camera.desc: Understand how photons from sunlight are transformed into a camera photo

rgb.desc: Understand the origin of RGB values and what they represent

aspect_ratio.desc: Understand the basics of aspect ratios and where they come from

decint-rep.desc: Understand the base-10 hindu-arabic representation of numbers

octal-rep.desc: Understand the base-8 representation of the natural numbers

hex-rep.desc: Understand the base-16 representation of the natural numbers, and how base-16 can be used to create colors.

binary-rep.desc: Understand the base-2 representation of the natural numbers. Define binary applications, addition, and be able to convert between the representation forms.

bits_bytes.desc: Understand the basics of computer memory, bits and bytes, and how to translate bits into characters, integers, etc.

bitmap.desc: Understand how to construct a bitmap from hex colors in C++ and how to parse bitmaps in C++.

bitmap_transforms.desc: Relate the idea of bitmap manipulation to the idea of transformations in linear algebra. Understand the very basics of vector manipulation.

pylists.desc: Understand how to use python lists and modify their contents

add_sub_polys.desc: Be able to add and subtract polynomials

mult_mono.desc: Be able to multiply monomials be each other, and a monomial by a polynomial

derivatives.desc: Understand the concept of a derivative and how to find it

power_rule.desc: Understand how to use the power rule

pyclasses.desc: Understand how to make a class in python and how to use them in Object Oriented Programming

poly_diff_squares.desc: Understand how to use the difference of squares formula

poly_perfect_squares.desc: Understand how to factor perfect squares

factoring.desc: Understand the process of factoring a polynomial

interp_poly.desc: Understand how to create a polynomial function given a set of points

rational_funcs.desc: Understand how to graph and simplify rational functions.

asymptotes.desc: Understand asymptotes, how to identify them visually as well as in an equation.

pfd.desc: Understand partial fraction decomposition and how to use it with factoring

limits.desc: Understand limits and how to find one, and how to find the end behavior of a function. Additionally, Understand how continuity relates to limits and behavior.

IVT.desc: Understand the definition of the intermediate value theorem, and how to apply it.

squeeze.desc: Understand the purpose of the squeeze theorem.

continuity.desc: Be able to define and determine continuity.

differentiation.desc: Define a derivative as a slope of a tangent line using limits. Additionally, be able to find if a point is differentiable.

lhopital_rule.desc: Be able to apply l Hospital's rule to indeterminate forms

mvt.desc: Be able to use the mean value theorem

product.desc: Apply the product rule to a function to find its derivative.

quotient.desc: Apply the quotient rule to a function to find its derivative.

chain.desc: Apply the chain rule to a function to find its derivative.

concavity.desc: Understand how determine concavity, inflection points, and use the second derivative test.
concavity.chap_title: First and Second Derivatives and the Shape of a Function

minmax.desc: Understand how to find local and global min/max values
minmax.chap_title: First and Second Derivatives and the Shape of a Function

critval.desc: Understand how to find critical values of a function
critval.chap_title: First and Second Derivatives and the Shape of a Function

programming-optimization.desc: Optimizing a best possible solution given a problem with many variables.

implicit_diff.desc: Understand how to use implicit differentiation to find derivatives with respect to x, vertical and horizontal tangents, and other rates of change.

related_rates.desc: Understand how to solve related rates problems using differentiation

multiv_func.desc: Understand how to evaluate functions of several variables, and be able to take their limits to determine continuity and differentiablity

partial_derivatves.desc: Understand how to calculate partial derivatves.

chain_partial.desc: Understand how to use the chain rule for partial derivatives

gradient_directional.desc: Understand gradient vectors and directional derivatives and their relation

linear_alg_intro.desc: Understand the concept of linear algebra, matrices, multiplying vector quantities, etc

matrix.desc: Understand matrices, vector representations of matrices, dimensions of matrices, scalar matrix multiplication, and the zero matrix and identity matrix.

matrix_oper.desc: Understand how to add, subtract, and multiplication matrices

row_col_major.desc: Understand the basics of how transposition can be simplified using row major or column major order. Relate this idea to computer memory

rref.desc: Transform systems of equations into matrices. Understand Gaussian and Gauss-Jordan Elimination, Row Echelon Form, and Reduced Row Echelon Form. Identify free variables, dimension, basics of basis.

matrices_applied.desc: Understand how to apply matrices and systems of linear equations to real life situations
matrices_applied.chap_title: Applications of Matrices in System Form

dependence.desc: Determine whether a set of vectors are independent or independent. Understand the basics of a basis and span.

subspaces.desc: Be able to identify and understand the fundamental subspaces: nullspace, rowspace, and columnspace.

nullspace.desc: Understand what a nullspace is, its properties, how to find it, and what it can tell us about a matrix A.

span.desc: Be able to determine the span of vectors in either a line, a plane, or all numbers

determinant.desc: Be able to find the determinant and understand what different values of it mean

lin_inverse_matrix.desc: Be able to to find inverses of matrices, and use them for practical vector problems.

cross.desc: Understand the cross product of two vectors

matrices_transformations.desc: Understand the idea that matrices can be represented as transformations. Be able to rotate, reflect, and scale basis or points with matrices. Begin thinking about eigenvectors.

eigen.desc: Understand how to compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors

projections.desc: Understand vector projections, what they create, and how to calculate them using matrices

gram-schmidt.desc: Understand how to use the gram-schmidt process for vectors

svd.desc: Understand how to use SVD in both in mathematics and python.

positive-semidefinite.desc: Understand how to calculate positive semidefinite values
positive-semidefinite.chap_title: Tackling Difficult Problems: Positive Semidefinite Matrices

pandas.desc: Understand how to use pandas to parse a CSV file in python

sql.desc: Understand how to parse tables in sql.

http.desc: Understand how HTTP requests work and their purpose.

decompression.desc: Understand how files are compressed and decompressed

html.desc: Understand HTML language, its typical usage, and how to work with a basic HTML document

newline.desc: Understand how to use the newline and carriage return characters.

utf-8.desc: Understand the basics of UTF-8 and advantages over ascii.

stemming_lemmatization.desc: Understand the processes of stemming and lemmatization.

accents.desc: Understand how different texts work with alphabets and different languages with symbolic letters. Differentiate between encodings versus alphabets.

matplotlib.desc: Understand how to use matplotlib in Python to plot graphs.

geocoding.desc: Understand the process of geocoding and reverse geocoding.

plotly.desc: Use python to make a geographical world map and add locations of longitude and latitude on it.

probability_independent.desc: Understand how to find probability of independent events. Understand that all probabilities must sum to 1.

probability_dependent.desc: Understand the basis of conditional probability and how to find the probability of two dependent events .

correlation_causation.desc: Understand the concept of "correlation does not imply causation".

uniform_distribution.desc: Understand the concept of uniform distribution. Recall bell curve from previous chapters.

combinatorics.desc: Understand how to complete combinatorics problems. Understand the binomial distribution.

factorial.desc: Understand how the factorial operation works.

permutations.desc: Understand the process of permutations, how to find them and how to apply them to sorting problems.

probability_conditional.desc: Understand how to calculate conditional probability. Be able to use the chain rule for probability

bayes.desc: Understand Bayes' theorem and be able to apply it to new information

monty-hall-prob.desc: Understand the monty hall problem and the application of Bayes' theorem behind it.

antiderivatives.desc: Understand how to find general antiderivatives, inverse derivative rules, and find general solutions and specific solutions. Find indefinite integrals

riemann.desc: Understand how to calculate riemann sums and area under the curve.

definite_integral.desc: Understand how to calculate definite integrals and their properties.

ftc_avg_val.desc: Understand and apply both parts of the FTC, and be able to evaluate definite integrals. Also, understand how to find the average value of a function.

arc_length.desc: Use integration to calculate the length of a curve or arc. Understand how to integrate for both 2D and 3D functions.

cdf_pdf.desc: Understand how to interpret continuous uniform distributions, cumulative probability functions, and probability density functions.

gas-temp.desc: How the temperature of a gas relates to the speed of the molecules

abs-zero.desc: Be able to define Absolute zero and the Kelvin temperature scale

ideal-gas-law.desc: Understand the Ideal Gas Law, Boyle's law, and Charles Law.

temp kinetic.desc: Understand molar heat capacity, adiabatic compression and decompression, and calculate temperature change.
temp kinetic.chap_title: Kinetic Energy and Temperature of a Gas

engine.desc: Understand how piston engines work, how they generate fuel and their side effects, and how to use engine terminology.

usub.desc: Understand how to integrate functions using u-sub.

polar_calc_derivative.desc: Understand how to find slopes of polar functions.

polar_calc_integrals.desc: Understand how to find areas of general polar functions.

diff_eqs.desc: Be able to solve ordinary differential equations, logistic DE's, and separable differential equations.

slope_fields.desc: Be able to work with slope fields, both graphing and identifying.

euler_method.desc: Be able to apply euler's method to approximate differential equations.

sequences2.desc: Be able to find the limit of a series. Define convergence and divergence

series_calc.desc: Understand how to use sequences to form series. Determine convergence and divergence of series.

series_tests.desc: Understand how to apply the series tests, including TFD, Integral Test, Ratio Test, both Comparison Tests, and the Root Test.

power_series.desc: Be able to use calculate the radius and intervals of convergence. Use calculus to approximate power series integrals and derivatives.

pop_prop.desc: Understand how the binomial distribution models sample outcomes when the true population is known. Be able to use the beta distribution.

normal_distribution.desc: Understand how to find the PDF of a normal distribution, and why it is important.

change_var.desc: Understand how changing variables effects a PDF and CDF

volume_single_int.desc: Understand how to find volumes using single integrals and solids of revolution around x, y, and other axis.

volume_double_integral\n.desc: Be able to find volumes of regions using double integrals using iterated integration. Understand fubini's theorem.

double_int_regions.desc: Be able to evaluate integrals over non-rectangular regions.

double_int_applications.desc: Be able to use double integrals to apply to real life problems including lamina densities, total mass, center of mass, and surface area.

multiv_distributions.desc: Be able to create probability distributions for functions relying on multiple variables.

paradoxes.desc: Understand what a paradox is, why they cause problems, and learn about a few types of paradoxes.
paradoxes.chap_title: Paradoxes, Thought Experiments, and the Illusions

multiv_normal.desc: Understand how to process different multi-variable normal distribution.

numerical_double_int.desc: Be able to use integration (and double integration) to approximate probability density functions.

graphs-programming.desc: Understand how graphs, nodes, and edges work together to form connections between data. Understand how weighted edges effect graphs.

dijkstra.desc: Understand how to use dikstra's algorithm to find the shortest path.

binary_search.desc: Implement binary search to search though lists, graphs, trees, and queues. Understand the requirements for a list to get binary searched.

graph_algorithms.desc: Understand how the processes of DFS and Bellman-Ford algorithms work.

bayes_classifier.desc: Understand how to use multivariate and Gaussian distributions to create bayes classifiers.
